expected_behavior: Dispatches 5 parallel verification agents covering distinct quality dimensions
Produces 0-10 scores across code quality, security, coverage, API, and UI
Calculates weighted composite score and letter grade (A-F)
Generates improvement suggestions with effort/impact prioritization

evaluations.query: Just run the tests for the user profile feature
expected_behavior: Runs backend and frontend test suites in parallel
Reports coverage data and pass/fail counts
Skips security audit and UI/UX analysis phases
Focuses on test-generator agent only for targeted test execution

evaluations.query: Run a security audit on the payment module
expected_behavior: Focuses on security-auditor agent for dedicated vulnerability scanning
Checks OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities against the codebase patterns
Validates secrets and credential handling follows secure storage practices
Applies blocking rules from policy-as-code if configured

evaluations.query: Quick check if my changes are ready to merge
expected_behavior: Runs tests only without detailed analysis
Skips nuanced grading and improvement suggestions
Provides simple pass/fail verdict without detailed scoring breakdown
Completes quickly compared to full verification

evaluations.query: How does the verify skill collect evidence for scoring code quality?
expected_behavior: Runs git diff and git log commands in parallel in one message for context gathering
Executes backend and frontend test suites in parallel with coverage reporting enabled
Uses --cov=app and --cov-report=json for machine-readable coverage data collection
Gathers evidence from multiple sources before producing any quality scores or grades

evaluations.query: What scoring scale does the verify skill use and what grades mean merge-ready?
expected_behavior: Uses 0 to 10 scale with decimals for nuanced scoring rather than binary pass or fail
Grades A (9-10) and B (7-8.9) are READY FOR MERGE while D and F are BLOCKED
Calculates composite score as weighted sum across multiple quality dimensions
Prioritizes improvements by impact divided by effort ratio to focus on high-value fixes
